behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves meaning, not just literal word substitution.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is decisive: correctness depends on real neural model output quality (grammar + semantic preservation), which a stub cannot validate. MOCK-HIDE applies because fixed mock outputs would always pass quality checks and hide real model regressions. Rejected higher level Workflow because behavior describes a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluation on a representative multilingual sentence set against the real translation model/API; score semantic adequacy and fluency (plus literal-translation negative cases), and fail if outputs are grammatical but meaning-distorting or overly literal beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: On translation API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and does not return an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (API client error -> fallback path -> response shaping), not one function. REAL-INFRA does not force System because a deterministic in-process stubbed 503 response can reproduce the failure boundary. Rejected System: behavior text specifies HTTP 503 handling, not real network timing/OS/subprocess conditions. Rejected Agentic: correctness here is routing on error, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + fallback translator + persistence in-process; make primary translator dependency return a 503 error object/response; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases existing in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on module interaction with local SQLite storage (querying seeded phrases), so Unit is too low for real schema/query/wiring failures. REAL-INFRA does not require System because this is local DB wiring, which fits Integration per level definitions. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model judgment involved.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite DB with known phrase pairs, call fallback translator through service/repository boundary, and assert exact translation returned for existing phrases (including normalization/case rules used by the app).

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: The compute+persist behavior crosses module boundaries (distance calculator -> metrics store), so pure Unit would miss storage wiring failures. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is deterministic algorithm correctness ('computes edit distance') and persistence, not evaluation of model output quality. Rejected System: no real network/OS/subprocess requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Provide deterministic primary/fallback strings with known edit-distance values, execute quality-tracking path end-to-end in-process, then verify persisted metric value and associated request/translation identifiers.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (primary result selection + async/background metric logging), beyond single-function Unit. Integration brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no dependence on model-output quality for pass/fail (can use fixed translator outputs); no multi-step agentic pipeline. Rejected System: behavior does not require real external infra to expose the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with deterministic primary and fallback translators plus a real in-process background executor/queue; assert immediate response uses primary translation, then assert quality-comparison log/metric record appears asynchronously with expected payload.
